summary: Markets exhibit mixed sentiment amid ongoing Iran conflict and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with crypto showing resilience and partial risk-off strength while commodities like gold remain supported. Forex pairs lean neutral with bearish biases, and Brent oil faces downward pressure below key resistance. Overall mood is cautious, prioritizing fundamental factors and upcoming economic data.

signals.name: Bitcoin
signals.reason: BTC holds above 74,000 confirming breakout from sideways range with targets at 80,000-85,000 amid risk-off resilience during Iran conflict.

signals.name: Ethereum
signals.reason: ETH returned +6% from conflict Day 0 outperforming S&P 500 and commodities, showing strong risk-off resilience.

signals.name: Gold
signals.reason: XAU/USD stays neutral-to-bullish above 4,750 near local highs despite geopolitical pressures.

signals.name: Brent Crude Oil
signals.reason: Brent remains neutral-to-bearish below 94-95 resistance under pressure from optimistic US statements despite Middle East risks.

signals.name: EUR/USD
signals.reason: EUR/USD neutral with bearish bias above 1.1600-1.1620 support amid sideways trend and potential Strait of Hormuz escalation.

signals.name: Solana
signals.reason: No specific mentions today amid broader crypto market edging higher by 1.8% in March despite tensions.

signals.name: XRP
signals.reason: Lacking direct commentary in recent posts with overall crypto outlook neutral due to geopolitical uncertainty.

signals.name: Chainlink
signals.reason: No targeted signals today as market focuses on BTC/ETH resilience in risk-off environment.

signals.name: Apple
signals.reason: Mag 7 stocks including AAPL down -10% from Iran conflict Day 0, underperforming crypto.

signals.name: Nvidia
